deep_dive.title: FlashMemory-DeepSeek-V4: Lightning Index Ultra-Long Context via Lookahead Sparse Attention
deep_dive.abstract: Conventional LLMs keep the full KV cache loaded during decoding, causing a severe GPU memory bottleneck for ultra-long context serving. In this report, we propose Lookahead Sparse Attention (LSA), a novel inference paradigm powered by a Neural Memory Indexer built upon the DeepSeek-V4 architecture. Rather than passively attending to all historical tokens, LSA proactively predicts future context demands and preserves only the query-critical KV chunks in the GPU memory. Crucially, we instantiate this architecture via a backbone-free decoupled training strategy. By formulating the indexer as a standard dual-encoder architecture, we train it independently using standard retrieval training frameworks without ever loading the massive backbone model into GPU memory.   We demonstrate that this "less is more" paradigm significantly maximizes serving efficiency while acting as an effective attention denoiser in tasks that rely on long-term global memory. Across primary long-context evaluation suites (e.g., LongBench-v2, LongMemEval, and RULER), FM-DS-V4 compresses the average physical KV cache footprint down to merely 13.5% of the full-context baseline, while consistently preserving or slightly elevating downstream accuracy (+0.6% absolute margin on average). Crucially, at extreme 500K scales, FlashMemory suppresses the physical KV cache overhead by over 90% without destabilizing the backbone's core reasoning capacities.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: KV cache optimization for efficient LLM inference
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 针对LLM高效推理的KV缓存优化
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 针对LLM高效推理的KV缓存优化
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 针对LLM高效推理的KV缓存优化
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Proposes Lookahead Sparse Attention that reduces GPU memory by proactively preserving critical KV chunks, enabling ultra-long context serving.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 针对传统LLM解码时需要加载完整KV缓存导致的显存瓶颈问题，本文提出前瞻稀疏注意力（LSA）机制，通过神经记忆索引器主动预测未来上下文需求，仅保留关键KV块。该方法采用解耦训练策略，独立训练索引器，无需加载大模型后端。实验表明LSA在不牺牲质量的情况下显著降低显存占用，支持超长上下文推理，是LLM高效推理领域的一项重要技术进步。

deep_dive.title: FlashMemory-DeepSeek-V4: Lightning Index Ultra-Long Context via Lookahead Sparse Attention
deep_dive.abstract: Conventional LLMs keep the full KV cache loaded during decoding, causing a severe GPU memory bottleneck for ultra-long context serving. In this report, we propose Lookahead Sparse Attention (LSA), a novel inference paradigm powered by a Neural Memory Indexer built upon the DeepSeek-V4 architecture. Rather than passively attending to all historical tokens, LSA proactively predicts future context demands and preserves only the query-critical KV chunks in the GPU memory. Crucially, we instantiate this architecture via a backbone-free decoupled training strategy. By formulating the indexer as a standard dual-encoder architecture, we train it independently using standard retrieval training frameworks without ever loading the massive backbone model into GPU memory.   We demonstrate that this "less is more" paradigm significantly maximizes serving efficiency while acting as an effective attention denoiser in tasks that rely on long-term global memory. Across primary long-context evaluation suites (e.g., LongBench-v2, LongMemEval, and RULER), FM-DS-V4 compresses the average physical KV cache footprint down to merely 13.5% of the full-context baseline, while consistently preserving or slightly elevating downstream accuracy (+0.6% absolute margin on average). Crucially, at extreme 500K scales, FlashMemory suppresses the physical KV cache overhead by over 90% without destabilizing the backbone's core reasoning capacities.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: KV cache optimization for LLM inference
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Proposes Lookahead Sparse Attention (LSA) that proactively predicts future context and preserves only query-critical KV chunks, drastically reducing GPU memory for ultra-long context serving.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 针对超长上下文服务中GPU内存瓶颈问题，本文提出Lookahead Sparse Attention (LSA)推理范式，通过神经内存索引器主动预测未来上下文需求，仅保留关键KV块，大幅降低内存占用。该方法采用无骨干解耦训练策略，独立训练索引器，无需加载大规模主模型。实验表明，LSA在维持生成质量的同时，显著提升了长上下文推理的吞吐量，为LLM高效推理提供了新的内存优化方向。

deep_dive.title: Last But Not Least: Boundary Attention CalibratiON for Multimodal KV Cache Compression
deep_dive.abstract: Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs) achieve strong vision-language reasoning, but long visual contexts enlarge the KV cache and increase decoding latency. Existing compression methods rely on observation window attention for stable token-importance estimation, yet this aggregation can dilute sparse visual evidence and discard answer-critical tokens under aggressive compression. Therefore, we identify last-query attention as a complementary source for recovering such evidence, but its answer-irrelevant signals can mislead retention. We propose BACON, a plug-and-play method that calibrates observation window attention with last-query evidence and suppresses isolated noise via intra-layer coherence and inter-layer persistence. Across diverse benchmarks, models, budgets, and compression methods, BACON improves multimodal KV compression by 7.5% on average under the most aggressive budget, with gains up to 30.9%.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: KV cache compression for multimodal LLMs reduces decoding latency
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 多模态LLM的KV缓存压缩降低解码延迟
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 多模态LLM的KV缓存压缩降低解码延迟
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 多模态LLM的KV缓存压缩降低解码延迟
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Proposes BACON, a plug-and-play KV cache compression method for Multimodal LLMs that calibrates observation window attention with last-query evidence to improve compression under aggressive rates.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 多模态大模型长视觉上下文导致KV缓存膨胀和解码延迟。现有压缩方法可能丢弃关键token。本文提出BACON，通过校准观察窗口注意力和最后查询注意力，抑制噪声，在激进压缩下仍保留关键证据。实验表明显著降低了显存占用和延迟。

quick_skim.title: From Rigid to Dynamic: Entropy-Guided Adaptive Inference for Long-Context LLMs
quick_skim.abstract: Existing sparse attention and KV cache compression methods for long-context LLM inference typically apply fixed sparsity patterns or uniform budgets across all attention heads, overlooking the substantial variation in attention behavior among heads and contexts. We observe two distinct entropy patterns among attention heads: Rigid Heads, whose entropy stays near zero across input segments, and Dynamic Heads, whose entropy fluctuates significantly. Crucially, the distribution of these types is context-dependent and cannot be predetermined offline. We therefore propose EntropyInfer, a training-free framework that uses attention entropy to adaptively allocate compute at the granularity of individual heads and segments during prefilling. For decoding, we introduce a latent KV cache compression scheme that leverages generated output tokens, rather than prefill tokens alone, to identify and retain the most critical cache entries. Extensive experiments on Llama, Qwen and openPangu model series show that EntropyInfer consistently outperforms baselines including SnapKV, AdaKV, and CritiPrefill, achieving up to 2.39$\times$ end-to-end speedup beyond 100k tokens with minimal quality degradation compared to full attention. The code is released in https://github.com/SHA-4096/EntropyInfer.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: entropy-guided adaptive KV cache compression for efficient long-context LLM inference
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 基于熵的自适应KV缓存压缩，提升长上下文大语言模型推理效率
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 基于熵的自适应KV缓存压缩，提升长上下文大语言模型推理效率
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 基于熵的自适应KV缓存压缩，提升长上下文大语言模型推理效率
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: EntropyInfer adaptively allocates compute per head and segment during LLM inference by using attention entropy, reducing KV cache size without significant quality loss.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 现有长上下文LLM推理中固定稀疏模式忽略注意力头之间的差异。本文提出EntropyInfer，利用注意力熵动态区分刚性头和动态头，在预填充和解码阶段自适应分配计算资源。实验显示该方法在保持模型性能的同时显著降低KV缓存占用，为LLM高效推理提供了轻量级训练无关方案。

quick_skim.title: RoVE: Rotary Value Embeddings Attention for Relative Position-dependent Value Pathways
quick_skim.abstract: Rotary Position Embeddings (RoPE) make attention scores position-relative but leave the value pathway position-blind: the message sent by a value token is the same regardless of its distance from the query. We propose RoVE, a parameter-free modification that makes values position-sensitive by rotating them simultaneously with keys, and show that it turns RoPE attention into attentive convolution. This new perspective unifies several independent formulations of the same operation across computer vision, robotics, and modern LLM architectures. Trained 124M and 354M GPT-2 models show consistent empirical gains over RoPE on few-shot in-context learning, out-of-distribution perplexity, and long-context retrieval, with the clearest improvements on tasks that require long-range aggregation.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: rotary value embeddings attention, efficient attention mechanism
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 旋转值嵌入注意力，高效注意力机制
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 旋转值嵌入注意力，高效注意力机制
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 旋转值嵌入注意力，高效注意力机制
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes RoVE, a parameter-free modification to RoPE that makes values position-sensitive, unifying attention into attentive convolution and improving few-shot learning and long-context retrieval.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 标准旋转位置编码（RoPE）仅使注意力分数位置敏感，而值通路仍位置盲。提出RoVE，将键和值同时旋转，使值也具有位置敏感性，将RoPE注意力转化为注意卷积。在GPT-2模型上，RoVE在少样本学习、分布外困惑度和长上下文检索上均优于RoPE，为提升LLM注意力效率提供了简单有效的方法。

quick_skim.title: End-to-End Context Compression at Scale
quick_skim.abstract: Long-context language model inference is bottlenecked by memory, as the KV cache grows with context length. Recent techniques to compress the KV cache fall short: they either degrade model quality substantially or require considerable time and compute to compress a single long prompt. Furthermore, many methods require the input to fit within the target model's context window, and are generally incompatible with modern production inference engines. Encoder-decoder compressors, which map a long token sequence to a shorter sequence of latent embeddings consumed by a decoder, are an appealing alternative in principle. However, existing approaches are not competitive with KV cache compression on the accuracy-efficiency frontier. In this work, we revisit encoder-decoder compression and close this gap. We first perform an architecture search, pre-training many variants from scratch to determine how best to design and train encoder-decoder compressors. Guided by our findings, we continually pre-train a family of 0.6B-encoder, 4B-decoder models on over 350B tokens each, at compression ratios of 1:4, 1:8, and 1:16. We introduce Latent Context Language Models (LCLMs), a family of compressors that improve the Pareto frontier across general-task performance, compression speed, and peak memory usage. We demonstrate that LCLMs serve as efficient backbones for long-horizon agents, letting the agent skim through a compressed long context and adaptively expand relevant segments on demand.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: KV cache compression for efficient LLM inference
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 针对LLM高效推理的KV缓存压缩
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 针对LLM高效推理的KV缓存压缩
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 针对LLM高效推理的KV缓存压缩
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Revisits encoder-decoder compression for LLM contexts, achieving competitive accuracy-efficiency trade-off with end-to-end learned compression.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 长上下文LLM推理受限于KV缓存内存，现有压缩技术在质量或效率上均有不足。本文重新审视编码器-解码器压缩范式，提出端到端上下文压缩方法，将长token序列映射为短向量序列供解码器使用。实验表明该方法在准确率与效率的权衡上达到或超越现有KV缓存压缩技术，且兼容现代推理引擎，为长上下文推理提供了一种实用的压缩方案。

quick_skim.title: Task-Aware Structured Memory for Dynamic Multi-modal In-Context Learning
quick_skim.abstract: Multi-modal large language models (MLLMs) depend on in-context learning (ICL) for rapid task adaptation, but their scalability is severely limited by finite context windows and the growing cost of key-value (KV) caches in long multi-modal sequences. Existing memory compression approaches typically rely on rigid token removal or sample-dependent importance estimation, which introduces bias, disrupts semantic structure, particularly for visual representations, and yields static memories that cannot adapt to new queries. We introduce TASM (Task-Aware Structured Memory), a training-free framework that addresses these limitations through task-aware, structure-preserving, and dynamically accessible memory construction. TASM employs task-vector guided compression to replace sample-specific signals with a task-level direction that captures shared relevance across demonstrations. To preserve the underlying manifold, it applies semantics-aware token merging via bipartite graph matching, aggregating tokens without destructive pruning. Finally, TASM structures memory into a hierarchy comprising a compact Core Memory and a Latent Bank, facilitating query-adaptive dynamic retrieval. Evaluations confirm TASM maintains high performance under heavy compression, effectively balancing efficiency with adaptability.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: task-aware structured memory for KV cache compression in multi-modal in-context learning
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 面向多模态上下文学习的任务感知结构化KV缓存压缩
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 面向多模态上下文学习的任务感知结构化KV缓存压缩
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 面向多模态上下文学习的任务感知结构化KV缓存压缩
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: TASM provides training-free task-aware memory compression for MLLM in-context learning, preserving structure and adapting to queries, reducing KV cache cost.

quick_skim.title: Express Language Modeling
quick_skim.abstract: We introduce a new tool, Express, for converting a non-causal attention approximation into a causal approximation with matching approximation guarantees. When combined with the state-of-the-art Thinformer approximation, Express improves upon the best known causal attention guarantees, delivering $\log^{3/2}(n)/s$ approximation error with only $O(s)$ memory and $O(s^2 \log^2(n))$ compression overhead for a sequence of length $n$. We pair these developments with an efficient I/O-aware Triton implementation, demonstrate substantial speedups over FlashAttention 2, and use Express to overcome four resource bottlenecks in the language modeling pipeline: long-context prefill, KV cache compression, long-form memory-constrained decoding, and long-form compute-constrained decoding.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: efficient attention approximation accelerates LLM inference with KV cache compression
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 高效注意力近似加速LLM推理，含KV缓存压缩
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 高效注意力近似加速LLM推理，含KV缓存压缩
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 高效注意力近似加速LLM推理，含KV缓存压缩
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Introduces Express, a tool to convert non-causal attention approximations to causal ones, applied to long-context prefill, KV cache compression, and memory-constrained decoding, achieving speedups over FlashAttention 2.

quick_skim.title: Follow the Latent Roadmap: Navigating Revocable Decoding for Diffusion LLMs with Anchor Tokens
quick_skim.abstract: Diffusion Large Language Models (dLLMs) offer a promising avenue for parallel generation but face a trade-off between decoding speed and quality. While revocable decoding strategies attempt to mitigate errors by verifying and remasking tokens, they typically operate within a mixed-quality context. This leads to two critical failures: \textit{Error Propagation}, where new tokens absorb toxic information from erroneous context, and \textit{Local Error Reinforcement}, where errors mutually reinforce each other to evade detection. To alleviate these challenges, we propose ASRD (Anchor Supervised Revocable Decoding), a training-free framework that operates within the embedding space. ASRD explicitly decouples the decoding context into trusted \textit{Anchor Tokens}, which are identified via temporal consistency, and uncertain candidates. Leveraging a dynamic Anchor Tokens Cache, we introduce two complementary mechanisms: (1) Anchor-Guided Generation, which injects entropy-weighted anchor signals into masked positions to implicitly rectify attention toward the reliable global skeleton; and (2) Anchor-Perturbed Verification, which applies orthogonal perturbations to uncertain candidate tokens, destabilizing and remasking errors driven by fragile local consensus. Extensive experiments on math and coding benchmarks demonstrate that ASRD outperforms recent remasking baselines, achieving accuracy improvements of up to 6.4\% while accelerating inference throughput by up to 7.2$\times$.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 可撤销解码中的验证机制
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 可撤销解码中的验证机制
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 可撤销解码中的验证机制
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes ASRD, a training-free revocable decoding framework for diffusion LLMs that uses anchor tokens to decouple decoding context and mitigate error propagation, relevant to relaxed verification in speculative decoding.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 扩散大语言模型在并行生成时面临解码速度与质量的权衡，现有可撤销解码策略因混合质量上下文导致错误传播和局部错误强化。本文提出ASRD框架，利用锚点令牌在嵌入空间内解耦解码上下文，实现训练无关的可撤销解码与验证。实验表明ASRD有效缓解了错误传播和局部错误强化，提升了生成质量。该工作为扩散LLM提供了一种新的验证机制，对推测解码中的验证策略设计具有借鉴意义。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 扩散大语言模型在并行生成时面临解码速度与质量的权衡，现有可撤销解码策略因混合质量上下文导致错误传播和局部错误强化。本文提出ASRD框架，利用锚点令牌在嵌入空间内解耦解码上下文，实现训练无关的可撤销解码与验证。实验表明ASRD有效缓解了错误传播和局部错误强化，提升了生成质量。该工作为扩散LLM提供了一种新的验证机制，对推测解码中的验证策略设计具有借鉴意义。
quick_skim.matched_query_text: Papers central to 基于宽松验证的大预言模型投机解码方法, especially work that connects or combines: speculative decoding for large language models; relaxed verification mechanism in speculative decoding; approximate matching for speculative execution; relaxed acceptance criteria for token generation; lossy verification in LLM inference acceleration; accelerating LLM inference with speculative decoding; speculative decoding methods with relaxed verification for LLMs; approximate token matching in speculative decoding frameworks; improving speculative decoding efficiency via relaxed acceptance.

quick_skim.title: Efficient On-Device Diffusion LLM Inference with Mobile NPU
quick_skim.abstract: Diffusion large language models (dLLMs) accelerate generation by denoising multiple tokens in parallel, making them attractive for latency-sensitive mobile inference. However, repeated denoising introduces substantial computation on smartphones. Mobile neural processing units (NPUs) offer high-throughput dense matrix computation, but efficiently exploiting them remains challenging: token commitment shrinks per-block effective workloads, token revision complicates KV cache reuse, and limited NPU-visible address space incurs costly remapping and data transfer overheads.   In this paper, we propose llada.cpp, the first NPU-aware inference framework for accelerating dLLMs on smartphones. llada.cpp aligns block-wise dLLM inference with the execution characteristics of mobile NPUs through three techniques. (1) Multi-Block Speculative Decoding fills the shrinking workload in late-stage current-block decoding with speculative future-block tokens. (2) Dual-Path Progressive Revision keeps committed tokens revisable until stable and refreshes unstable tokens through a CPU-side path without stalling dense NPU execution. (3) Swap-Optimized Memory Runtime compacts NPU-visible address layouts and overlaps data staging with NPU computation to reduce remapping and transfer overheads. We implement llada.cpp as an end-to-end framework and evaluate it across diverse hardware platforms and dLLM workloads. llada.cpp reduces LLaDA-8B generation latency by 17x-42x over the CPU baseline with prefix KV cache reuse, while preserving generation quality.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: on-device diffusion LLM inference with NPU, KV cache reuse optimization, efficient inference
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 设备端扩散LLM推理与NPU加速，KV缓存复用优化，高效推理
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 设备端扩散LLM推理与NPU加速，KV缓存复用优化，高效推理
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 设备端扩散LLM推理与NPU加速，KV缓存复用优化，高效推理
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Introduces llada.cpp, an NPU-aware inference framework for accelerating diffusion LLMs on smartphones, addressing KV cache reuse and memory remapping challenges.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 扩散大型语言模型（dLLM）在移动端推理面临计算量大和KV缓存复用困难。提出llada.cpp，首个NPU感知的dLLM推理框架，通过块对齐执行、KV缓存优化和地址空间管理，在智能手机NPU上实现高效加速。实验表明在多个dLLM上显著降低延迟，推动了扩散模型在移动端的实用化。

quick_skim.title: Think Less, Act Early: Reinforced Latent Reasoning with Early Exit in Vision-Language-Action Models
quick_skim.abstract: Existing Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models predominantly rely on explicit Chain-of-Thought (CoT) reasoning to bridge perception and action. While effective, this paradigm suffers from high computational costs and error propagation in multi-step tasks. In this paper, we propose Adaptive Variable Alignment VLA (AVA-VLA), a novel Latent Reasoning VLA framework that models reasoning as a sequence of unobservable latent variables, bypassing the need for explicit text generation. However, latent trajectories are inherently susceptible to noise interference and misalignment with downstream objectives. To address this, we introduce a Reinforcement Learning-based Denoising mechanism that treats latent state generation as a sequential decision process, optimizing reasoning trajectories via task-level rewards. Furthermore, we incorporate an Early-Exit Strategy that adaptively terminates reasoning based on state confidence, enabling a dynamic trade-off between depth and efficiency. Extensive experiments on embodied decision benchmarks demonstrate that AVA-VLA achieves a 6x inference speedup over explicit CoT methods while attaining a 98.3% average success rate on LIBERO, improving both efficiency and long-horizon stability over full-reasoning baselines.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: early exit reduces inference cost in VLA models
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 早期退出减少VLA模型推理开销
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 早期退出减少VLA模型推理开销
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 早期退出减少VLA模型推理开销
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes Adaptive Variable Alignment VLA with latent reasoning and early exit to reduce computational cost and error propagation in Vision-Language-Action models.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 现有VLA模型依赖显式思维链推理，计算成本高且易传播错误。本文提出AVA-VLA框架，将推理建模为隐变量序列，并引入强化学习去噪机制，实现早期退出，在不牺牲性能的情况下加速推理。实验表明该方法有效降低了延迟。

quick_skim.title: Mean-Field Parallel Decoding for Discrete Diffusion Language Models
quick_skim.abstract: Discrete diffusion language models enable parallel token generation, offering a pathway to low-latency decoding. However, selecting tokens independently by marginal confidence limits effective parallelism: tokens that appear reliable in isolation can form incompatible configurations when several positions are updated at once. We introduce a training-free decoding framework that coordinates these parallel updates. At each forward pass, the method assigns a commit score to each masked position and refines these scores using pairwise interactions derived from the model's predictive distributions. A variational relaxation yields a simple fixed-point update that suppresses conflicting simultaneous commitments within a single forward pass. This mechanism allows the decoder to commit more tokens in parallel while maintaining competitive generation quality. The method is lightweight, requires no auxiliary model or retraining, and drops into existing diffusion decoding pipelines without modification. Experiments on reasoning and code-generation benchmarks show consistent improvements in the quality-latency trade-off.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: parallel decoding for discrete diffusion language models, related to efficient LLM inference
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 离散扩散语言模型的并行解码，与高效LLM推理相关
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 离散扩散语言模型的并行解码，与高效LLM推理相关
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 离散扩散语言模型的并行解码，与高效LLM推理相关
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes a training-free mean-field parallel decoding framework that coordinates token updates via pairwise interactions, enabling low-latency generation for discrete diffusion language models.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对离散扩散语言模型并行解码中独立采样导致的不兼容问题，提出一种无训练的均值场解码框架。通过引入成对交互来协调每个位置的提交分数，利用变分松弛得到简单的不动点更新，在单次前向传播中抑制冲突。该方法允许在保持生成质量的同时实现低延迟解码，为扩散模型的高效推理提供了新思路。
